Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命}}, {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}} (Small), {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}} {{ill|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines|ja|三宮}}, Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Komonoi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}, Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}}, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社}}. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}} prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}} during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoimi Shrine]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Komonoimi Shrine]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[en:Komonoimi Shrine]]

revision.comment: 25 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:小物忌神社 up to rev 83984308
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|山形県酒田市'''山楯'''の小物忌神社|同市'''飛島'''の同名神社|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)}} {{神社 |名称 = 小物忌神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|小物忌神社　拝殿]]
小物忌神社　拝殿 |所在地 = 山形県酒田市山楯字三之宮48 |位置 = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|E|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=小物忌神社}} |祭神 = [[シナツヒコ|級長津比古命]]、[[シナツヒコ|級長津比賣命]]、[[トヨウケビメ|豊受比賣命]] |社格 = 式内社（小）・[[出羽国]][[一宮#二宮、三宮|三宮]]・旧県社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[8月17日]] |神事 = 天狗舞神楽 |地図={{Location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=小物忌神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} [[ファイル:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|一の鳥居
[[国道345号]]の交差点付近にある一の鳥居。]] [[ファイル:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|本殿]] [[ファイル:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|夢草堂]] '''小物忌神社'''（おものいみじんじゃ）は、[[山形県]][[酒田市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[出羽国]][[一宮#二宮、三宮|三宮]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。酒田市内には同名の神社が2カ所にある。 == 祭神 == * [[シナツヒコ|級長津比古命]] * [[シナツヒコ|級長津比賣命]] * [[トヨウケビメ|豊受比賣命]] == 由緒 == 創建年代は不詳。社伝では[[景行天皇]]時代に[[武内宿禰]]が官籍に載せたとされている。[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に刊行された『飽海郡誌　巻之六』によれば、もとは「三之宮大座明神」と称し、古来より[[鳥海山大物忌神社]]の第3王子とであるとされ、鳥海山大物忌神社が「春物忌」を行った後に当社においても3日間の「物忌」を行うのが慣わしになっていたと言う。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）の『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に小社として記載されているほか、[[元慶]]2年（[[878年]]）に[[従五位|従五位下]][[勲等|勲七等]]の[[神階]]に昇叙され『[[日本三代実録]]』8月4日条。、翌々年の同4年（[[880年]]）に更に従五位上に昇叙された『日本三代実録』2月27日条。「袁物忌神」に相当すると見られている。 『山形縣神社誌』山形県神社庁五十周年記念事業実行委員会出版部 編　『山形縣神社誌』　山形県神社庁　2000年4月　より。では、[[寛治]]5年（[[1091年]]）[[源義家]]が東征の際、賊軍の勢いが強く戦が不利になった為、家臣の首藤主馬を大物忌神社と小物忌神社に使わして戦勝を祈願させた。やがて敵陣を陥落させた後、[[太刀|大刀]]と[[鏑矢]]を奉納した、との社伝を紹介している。 16世紀前期には、この地方の領主[[武藤氏]]一門の[[砂越城|砂越（さごし）城主]]に[[社領]]を没収され、[[天正|天正年間]]には兵火により社殿が焼失し荒廃していた。『風土略記』には、兵火に罹って社殿神体が焼失し、残った台座を新殿に安置したので「大座明神」と呼ばれた、との言い伝えが記載されている。しかしながら『風土略記』では、この説の真偽は不明であり、社名の由来としては不敬な称呼ではないか、吹浦の古い縁起にも大座とあるので、兵火による焼失後の呼称では無いだろうと考察している。 [[江戸時代]]に入り[[元文]]元年（[[1736年]]）[[庄内藩]]主[[酒井氏]]により復興された。『出羽風土略記』によれば、[[社家]]も無く、三之宮村の村役人が守護してきたが、山楯村の長久寺が[[別当]]となって[[宝暦]]9年（[[1759年]]）経堂を建て、1宇の仏壇を構えて念仏修行の場としたと言う。さらに観音とのみ称して社号を捨てたため、公儀の諸国神社調査からも洩れてしまったとされる。『日本の神々 -神社と聖地-　12　東北・北海道』谷川健一 編　『日本の神々 -神社と聖地-　12　東北・北海道』　㈱白水社　1984年6月　より。においても、再建はされたが長久寺の力が強く、念仏道場として観音と呼ばれていた、と述べている。 『飽海郡誌　巻之六』によれば、[[明治]]9年（[[1876年]]）に新たに3神を祭り、初めて小物忌神社と公称した。同年[[2月24日]][[村社]]に列格、同年[[11月11日]][[郷社]]に列せられると同時に[[鳥海山大物忌神社|大物忌神社]]の[[摂社]]に指定された。しかしながら、明治32年（[[1899年]]）[[3月26日]]に社殿が焼失。すぐに再建に着手し、明治34年（[[1901年]]）[[11月24日]]に竣功、遷宮式を行った。明治35年（[[1902年]]）[[3月25日]][[県社]]に列せられた、としている。 === 年表 === * [[景行天皇]]時代 - [[武内宿禰]]により官籍に記録 * [[元慶]]2年（[[878年]]）8月 - 従五位下勲七等に昇叙 *:[[元慶の乱|秋田夷乱（元慶の乱）]]において朝廷軍が敗退したのを受け占ったところ、古来より征戦に霊験を有する[[鳥海山大物忌神社|大物忌神]]、[[出羽三山|月山神]]、小物忌神の3神が、神気賊に帰して祈祷が届かなくなってしまったと出た。そこで3社の爵級を増せば霊応あるべしとして、小物忌神は勳九等を勳七等に進められた。 * 元慶4年（[[880年]]） - 従五位上に昇叙 * [[寛治]]5年（[[1091年]]） - 源義家戦勝祈願と伝えられる * 明治9年（1876年）2月24日 - 村社 * 同年[[11月11日]] - 郷社、[[鳥海山大物忌神社|大物忌神社]][[摂社]] * 明治35年（[[1902年]]）[[3月25日]] - 県社 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[羽越本線]][[砂越駅]]から自動車で約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *[[佐伯有義]] 編　『神祇全書　第1輯』　[[皇典講究所]] 1906年10月　（1971年に思文閣より複製版が出されている） *[[谷川健一]] 編　『日本の神々 -神社と聖地-　12　東北・北海道』　[[白水社|㈱白水社]]　1984年6月 *神道大系編纂会編　『[[神道大系]]　神社編28　出羽国』　神道大系編纂会　1990年3月 *山形県神社庁五十周年記念事業実行委員会出版部 編　『山形縣神社誌』　山形県神社庁　2000年4月 == 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} *[[小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] - 酒田市内にある同名神社。同じく[[式内社論社]]とされる。 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.omonoimi.com/ 小物忌神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おものいみしんしや}} [[Category:山形県の神社]] [[Category:出羽国の式内社]] [[Category:山形県の旧県社]] [[Category:酒田市の歴史]] [[Category:出羽国|社3]] [[Category:酒田市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|山形県酒田市'''山楯'''の小物忌神社|同市'''飛島'''の同名神社|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)}} {{神社 |名称 = 小物忌神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|小物忌神社　拝殿]]
小物忌神社　拝殿 |所在地 = 山形県酒田市山楯字三之宮48 |位置 = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|E|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=小物忌神社}} |祭神 = [[シナツヒコ|級長津比古命]]、[[シナツヒコ|級長津比賣命]]、[[トヨウケビメ|豊受比賣命]] |社格 = 式内社（小）・[[出羽国]][[一宮#二宮、三宮|三宮]]・旧県社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[8月17日]] |神事 = 天狗舞神楽 |地図={{Location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=小物忌神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} [[ファイル:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|一の鳥居
[[国道345号]]の交差点付近にある一の鳥居。]] [[ファイル:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|本殿]] [[ファイル:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|夢草堂]] '''小物忌神社'''（おものいみじんじゃ）は、[[山形県]][[酒田市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[出羽国]][[一宮#二宮、三宮|三宮]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は県社。酒田市内には同名の神社が2カ所にある。 == 祭神 == * [[シナツヒコ|級長津比古命]] * [[シナツヒコ|級長津比賣命]] * [[トヨウケビメ|豊受比賣命]] == 由緒 == 創建年代は不詳。社伝では[[景行天皇]]時代に[[武内宿禰]]が官籍に載せたとされている。[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に刊行された『飽海郡誌　巻之六』によれば、もとは「三之宮大座明神」と称し、古来より[[鳥海山大物忌神社]]の第3王子とであるとされ、鳥海山大物忌神社が「春物忌」を行った後に当社においても3日間の「物忌」を行うのが慣わしになっていたと言う。 [[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）の『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に小社として記載されているほか、[[元慶]]2年（[[878年]]）に[[従五位|従五位下]][[勲等|勲七等]]の[[神階]]に昇叙され『[[日本三代実録]]』8月4日条。、翌々年の同4年（[[880年]]）に更に従五位上に昇叙された『日本三代実録』2月27日条。「袁物忌神」に相当すると見られている。 『山形縣神社誌』山形県神社庁五十周年記念事業実行委員会出版部 編　『山形縣神社誌』　山形県神社庁　2000年4月　より。では、[[寛治]]5年（[[1091年]]）[[源義家]]が東征の際、賊軍の勢いが強く戦が不利になった為、家臣の首藤主馬を大物忌神社と小物忌神社に使わして戦勝を祈願させた。やがて敵陣を陥落させた後、[[太刀|大刀]]と[[鏑矢]]を奉納した、との社伝を紹介している。 16世紀前期には、この地方の領主[[武藤氏]]一門の[[砂越城|砂越（さごし）城主]]に[[社領]]を没収され、[[天正|天正年間]]には兵火により社殿が焼失し荒廃していた。『風土略記』には、兵火に罹って社殿神体が焼失し、残った台座を新殿に安置したので「大座明神」と呼ばれた、との言い伝えが記載されている。しかしながら『風土略記』では、この説の真偽は不明であり、社名の由来としては不敬な称呼ではないか、吹浦の古い縁起にも大座とあるので、兵火による焼失後の呼称では無いだろうと考察している。 [[江戸時代]]に入り[[元文]]元年（[[1736年]]）[[庄内藩]]主[[酒井氏]]により復興された。『出羽風土略記』によれば、[[社家]]も無く、三之宮村の村役人が守護してきたが、山楯村の長久寺が[[別当]]となって[[宝暦]]9年（[[1759年]]）経堂を建て、1宇の仏壇を構えて念仏修行の場としたと言う。さらに観音とのみ称して社号を捨てたため、公儀の諸国神社調査からも洩れてしまったとされる。『日本の神々 -神社と聖地-　12　東北・北海道』谷川健一 編　『日本の神々 -神社と聖地-　12　東北・北海道』　㈱白水社　1984年6月　より。においても、再建はされたが長久寺の力が強く、念仏道場として観音と呼ばれていた、と述べている。 『飽海郡誌　巻之六』によれば、[[明治]]9年（[[1876年]]）に新たに3神を祭り、初めて小物忌神社と公称した。同年[[2月24日]][[村社]]に列格、同年[[11月11日]][[郷社]]に列せられると同時に[[鳥海山大物忌神社|大物忌神社]]の[[摂社]]に指定された。しかしながら、明治32年（[[1899年]]）[[3月26日]]に社殿が焼失。すぐに再建に着手し、明治34年（[[1901年]]）[[11月24日]]に竣功、遷宮式を行った。明治35年（[[1902年]]）[[3月25日]][[県社]]に列せられた、としている。 === 年表 === * [[景行天皇]]時代 - [[武内宿禰]]により官籍に記録 * [[元慶]]2年（[[878年]]）8月 - 従五位下勲七等に昇叙 *:[[元慶の乱|秋田夷乱（元慶の乱）]]において朝廷軍が敗退したのを受け占ったところ、古来より征戦に霊験を有する[[鳥海山大物忌神社|大物忌神]]、[[出羽三山|月山神]]、小物忌神の3神が、神気賊に帰して祈祷が届かなくなってしまったと出た。そこで3社の爵級を増せば霊応あるべしとして、小物忌神は勳九等を勳七等に進められた。 * 元慶4年（[[880年]]） - 従五位上に昇叙 * [[寛治]]5年（[[1091年]]） - 源義家戦勝祈願と伝えられる * 明治9年（1876年）2月24日 - 村社 * 同年[[11月11日]] - 郷社、[[鳥海山大物忌神社|大物忌神社]][[摂社]] * 明治35年（[[1902年]]）[[3月25日]] - 県社 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[羽越本線]][[砂越駅]]から自動車で約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *[[佐伯有義]] 編　『神祇全書　第1輯』　[[皇典講究所]] 1906年10月　（1971年に思文閣より複製版が出されている） *[[谷川健一]] 編　『日本の神々 -神社と聖地-　12　東北・北海道』　[[白水社|㈱白水社]]　1984年6月 *神道大系編纂会編　『[[神道大系]]　神社編28　出羽国』　神道大系編纂会　1990年3月 *山形県神社庁五十周年記念事業実行委員会出版部 編　『山形縣神社誌』　山形県神社庁　2000年4月 == 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} *[[小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)]] - 酒田市内にある同名神社。同じく[[式内社論社]]とされる。 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.omonoimi.com/ 小物忌神社]（神社公式） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おものいみしんしや}} [[Category:山形県の神社]] [[Category:出羽国の式内社]] [[Category:山形県の旧県社]] [[Category:酒田市の歴史]] [[Category:出羽国|社3]] [[Category:酒田市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, '''Yamadate''' Komonoi Shrine|The shrine with the same name on '''Tobishima''' in the same city|Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命}}, {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}} (Small), {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}} {{ill|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines|ja|三宮}}, Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Komonoi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県}}, Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国}}, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}} during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社}}. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家}} prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏}} during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoimi Shrine]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Komonoimi Shrine]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[en:Komonoimi Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Otheruses|Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, '''Yamadate''' Komonoi Shrine|The shrine with the same name on '''Tobishima''' in the same city|Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|12=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (Small), {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|12=draft}} {{ill|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines|ja|三宮}}, Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|12=draft}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Komonoi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|12=draft}}, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=draft}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社|12=draft}}. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|12=draft}} prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏|12=draft}} during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線|12=draft}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoimi Shrine]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Komonoimi Shrine]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[en:Komonoimi Shrine]]

Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Komonoi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft}}, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰|qq=draft}} during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社|qq=draft}}. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft}}'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft}} prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏|qq=draft}} during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線|qq=draft}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoimi Shrine]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Komonoimi Shrine]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[en:Komonoimi Shrine]]

It is the third shrine (三宮) in {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft}}, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰|qq=draft}} during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社|qq=draft}}. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft}}'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft}} prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏|qq=draft}} during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線|qq=draft}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[en:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]

Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Komonoi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Province|qq=}}, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|qq=}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|qq=}} during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|qq=}}. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai clan|qq=}} during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR|qq=}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uetsu Main Line|qq=}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[en:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]

It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai clan|qq=}} during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR|qq=}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uetsu Main Line|qq=}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[en:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]

Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Small), {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Province|qq=}} {{ill|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines|ja|三宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines|qq=}}, Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengu Mai Kagura|qq=}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Komonoi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Province|qq=}}, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|qq=}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰|1=Takenouchi no Sukune|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|qq=}} during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|qq=}}. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Small), {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Province|qq=}} {{ill|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines|ja|三宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines|qq=}}, Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengu Mai Kagura|qq=}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Komonoi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakata|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dewa Province|qq=}}, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|qq=}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰|1=Takenouchi no Sukune|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi no Sukune|qq=}} during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|qq=}}. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoshiie|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai clan|qq=}} during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR|qq=}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uetsu Main Line|qq=}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]}} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[en:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]

It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]}} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[en:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Small), [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]] [[:en:Sannomiya|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines]], Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengu Mai Kagura|qq=}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Komonoi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|qq=}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]] during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]]. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]] during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]}} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[en:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[Category:Dewa Province]]

Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]}} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[en:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Small), [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]] [[:en:Sannomiya|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines]], Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengu Mai Kagura|qq=}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Komonoi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|qq=}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]] during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]]. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]] during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]}} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[en:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:酒田市の建築物]]

The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]}} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[en:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:酒田市の建築物]] [[Category:酒田市の歴史]]
