revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Small), [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]] [[:en:Sannomiya|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines]], Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengu Mai Kagura|qq=}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Komonoi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|qq=}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]] during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]]. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]] during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]}} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[en:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:酒田市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Sakata]]

Rebuilding efforts were supported by the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]] during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]}} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakata]] [[Category:History of Sakata]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|Komonoi Shrine Haiden]]
Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (Small), [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]] [[:en:Sannomiya|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines]], Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengu Mai Kagura|qq=}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Komonoi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|qq=}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]] during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]]. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]] during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]}} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakata]] [[Category:History of Sakata]] {{Commons category|Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate}}

After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]] during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]}} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakata]] [[Category:History of Sakata]] {{Commons category|Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate}}

Komonoi Shrine Haiden |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Komonoi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Small), [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]] [[:en:Sannomiya|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines]], Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengu Mai Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Komonoi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]] during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]]. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]] during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]}} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakata]] [[Category:History of Sakata]] {{Commons category|Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate}}

The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]] during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]}} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakata]] [[Category:History of Sakata]] {{Commons category|Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate}}

It is the third shrine (三宮) in [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]] during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]]. It is the third shrine (三宮) in [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]] during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]]. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]] during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]}} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakata]] [[Category:History of Sakata]] {{Commons category|Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11462862]] Wikidata name: Omonoimi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38.899167|N|139.941389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|qid=Q1073925}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Small), [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]] [[:en:Sannomiya|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines]], Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengu Mai Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}|prefecture= {{ill|Sakata|qid=Q504690}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Komonoi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]] during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]]. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]] during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]}} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakata]] [[Category:History of Sakata]] {{Commons category|Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate}} [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11462862]] Wikidata name: Omonoimi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38.899167|N|139.941389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|qid=Q1073925}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Small), [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]] [[:en:Sannomiya|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines]], Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengu Mai Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}|prefecture= {{ill|Sakata|qid=Q504690}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Komonoi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]] during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]]. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]] during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]]}} [[sn:Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakata]] [[Category:History of Sakata]] {{Commons category|Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate}} {{wikidata link|Q11462862}}

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate]][[ja:小物忌神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11462862]] Wikidata name: Omonoimi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38.899167|N|139.941389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|qid=Q1073925}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Small), [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]] [[:en:Sannomiya|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines]], Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengu Mai Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}|prefecture= {{ill|Sakata|qid=Q504690}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Komonoi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]] during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]]. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]] during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakata]] [[Category:History of Sakata]] {{Commons category|Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate}} {{wikidata link|Q11462862}}

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate]][[ja:小物忌神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11462862]] Wikidata name: Omonoimi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38.899167|N|139.941389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|WD=Q1073925}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Small), [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]] [[:en:Sannomiya|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines]], Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengu Mai Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}|prefecture= {{ill|Sakata|WD=Q504690}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Komonoi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]] during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]]. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]] during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakata]] [[Category:History of Sakata]] {{Commons category|Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate}} {{wikidata link|Q11462862}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate]][[ja:小物忌神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11462862]] Wikidata name: Omonoimi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komonoi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38.899167|N|139.941389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|WD=Q1073925}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Small), [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]] [[:en:Sannomiya|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines]], Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengu Mai Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Komonoi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}|prefecture= {{ill|Sakata|WD=Q504690}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Komonoi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Sakata, Yamagata|Sakata]], [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in [[:en:Dewa Province|Dewa Province]], and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-bime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmonoimi no Kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by [[:en:Takenouchi no Sukune|Takenouchi no Sukune]] during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of [[:en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]]. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshiie|Minamoto no Yoshiie]] prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the [[:en:Sakai clan|Sakai clan]] during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Uetsu Main Line|Uetsu Main Line]] Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{reflist}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omonoimi Shrine (Tobishima, Sakata)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[Category:Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakata]] [[Category:History of Sakata]] {{Commons category|Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate}} {{wikidata link|Q11462862}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰|qq=draft}} during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社|qq=draft}}. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft}}'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft}} prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏|qq=draft}} during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Komonoi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線|qq=draft}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Komonoi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Komonoi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Komonoimi Shrine]] [[Category:Cgoodbye $1tier 5]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Komonoimi Shrine]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[sn:omonoimi Shrine]] [[en:Komonoimi Shrine]]
