revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Omonoimi Shrine |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|Omonoimi Shrine Haiden]]
Omonoimi Shrine Haiden |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Omonoimi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} (Small), {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines|ja|三宮}}, Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|qq=draft}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Omonoimi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} '''Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Dewa Province]]. It is a candidate for {{ill|Womonoimino Shrine|lt=Womonoimino Shrine|WD=Q135040052}}.[https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182139 DB][[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Omonoimi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft}}, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰|qq=draft}} during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社|qq=draft}}. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft}}'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft}} prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏|qq=draft}} during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Omonoimi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線|qq=draft}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Omonoimi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{wikidata link|Q11462862}}{{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[co:commons:Category:Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with contradicting interwikis]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線|qq=draft}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Omonoimi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{wikidata link|Q11462862}}{{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[co:commons:Category:Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with contradicting interwikis]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Omonoimi Shrine |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|Omonoimi Shrine Haiden]]
Omonoimi Shrine Haiden |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Omonoimi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} (Small), {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines|ja|三宮}}, Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|qq=draft}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Omonoimi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} '''Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Dewa Province]]. It is a candidate for {{ill|Womonoimino Shrine|lt=Womonoimino Shrine|WD=Q135040052}}.[https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182139 DB][[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Omonoimi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft}}, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰|qq=draft}} during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社|qq=draft}}. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft}}'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft}} prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏|qq=draft}} during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Omonoimi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線|qq=draft}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Omonoimi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{wikidata link|Q11462862}}{{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[co:commons:Category:Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with contradicting interwikis]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線|qq=draft}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Omonoimi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{wikidata link|Q11462862}}{{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[co:commons:Category:Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with contradicting interwikis]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Omonoimi Shrine |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|Omonoimi Shrine Haiden]]
Omonoimi Shrine Haiden |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Omonoimi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} (Small), {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines|ja|三宮}}, Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|qq=draft}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Omonoimi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} '''Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Dewa Province]]. It is a candidate for {{ill|Womonoimino Shrine|lt=Womonoimino Shrine|WD=Q135040052|1=Womonoimino Shrine}}.[https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182139 DB][[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Omonoimi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft}}, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰|qq=draft}} during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社|qq=draft}}. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft}}'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft}} prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏|qq=draft}} during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Omonoimi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線|qq=draft}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Omonoimi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{wikidata link|Q11462862}}{{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[co:commons:Category:Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with contradicting interwikis]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線|qq=draft}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Omonoimi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{wikidata link|Q11462862}}{{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[co:commons:Category:Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with contradicting interwikis]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Omonoimi Shrine |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|Omonoimi Shrine Haiden]]
Omonoimi Shrine Haiden |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Omonoimi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} (Small), {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines|ja|三宮}}, Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|qq=draft}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Omonoimi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} '''Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Dewa Province]]. It is a candidate for {{ill|Womonoimino Shrine|lt=Womonoimino Shrine|WD=Q135040052|1=Womonoimino Shrine}}.[https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182139 DB][[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Omonoimi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft}}, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰|qq=draft}} during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社|qq=draft}}. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft}}'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft}} prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏|qq=draft}} during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Omonoimi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線|qq=draft}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Omonoimi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{wikidata link|Q11462862}}{{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[co:commons:Category:Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with contradicting interwikis]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線|qq=draft}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Omonoimi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{wikidata link|Q11462862}}{{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[co:commons:Category:Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[Category:Architecture in Sakata City]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Omonoimi Shrine |image = [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Haiden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|Omonoimi Shrine Haiden]]
Omonoimi Shrine Haiden |location = Yamagata Prefecture, Sakata City, Yamadate, Minomiya 48 |coordinates = {{Coord|38|53|57.0|N|139|56|29.0|scale:10000_region:JP-06_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Omonoimi Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} (Small), {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ichinomiya#Second and third shrines|ja|三宮}}, Former Prefectural Shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft}} |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Main Festival= August 17 |Rituals = {{ill|Tengu Mai Kagura|ja|天狗舞神楽|qq=draft}} |Map = {{location map|Japan Yamagata|lat_deg=38|lat_min=53|lat_sec=57.0|lon_deg=139|lon_min=56|lon_sec=29.0|label=Omonoimi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} '''Omonoimi Shrine (Yamadate, Sakata)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Dewa Province]]. It is a candidate for {{ill|Womonoimino Shrine|lt=Womonoimino Shrine|WD=Q135040052|1=Womonoimino Shrine}}.[https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182139 DB][[File:Omonoimi-jinja Gate(Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|First Torii
Located near the intersection of National Route 345.]] [[File:Omonoimi-jinja Honden (Yamadate).JPG|250px|right|thumb|Main Hall]] [[File:Musoudou.JPG|250px|right|thumb|Musoudou]] '''Omonoimi Shrine''' (小物忌神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Sakata|ja|酒田市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|ja|山形県|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is the third shrine (三宮) in {{ill|Dewa Province|ja|出羽国|qq=draft}}, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}} was Prefectural Shrine. There are two shrines with this name in Sakata City. == Deities == * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比古命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|lt=Shinatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|級長津比賣命|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Toyouke-bime|ja|豊受比賣命|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmonoimi no Kami|ja|大物忌神|qq=draft}}) == History == The exact founding date is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it was recorded by {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|ja|武内宿禰|qq=draft}} during the reign of Emperor Keikō. The 1913 ''Akumi County Gazetteer, Volume Six'' states that the shrine was originally called "San-no-Miya Dai-za Myōjin" and traditionally considered the third prince of {{ill|Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|ja|鳥海山大物忌神社|qq=draft}}. It was customary for this shrine to hold a three-day "monoimi" ritual following the "Harumonoimi" of Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. The shrine is listed as a small shrine (小社) in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}'' of 927. It was also promoted to the lower fifth Rank and seventh court Rank (従五位下勲七等) in 878''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft}}'', August 4 entry. and to the upper fifth Rank in 880.''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'', February 27 entry. It is believed to correspond to the deity "Ōmonoimi-no-Kami." In ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association, April 2000., it is noted that during the fifth year of Kanji (1091), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|ja|源義家|qq=draft}} prayed for victory at this shrine through his retainer Sutō Shuma. After achieving victory, he dedicated a sword and arrow. The shrine faced challenges in the early 16th century, including confiscation of shrine lands by the Sago Castle Lord of the Mutō clan and destruction by fire during the Tenshō era. Rebuilding efforts were supported by the {{ill|Sakai clan|ja|酒井氏|qq=draft}} during the Genbun era (1736). In 1876, three new deities were enshrined, and the name "Omonoimi Shrine" was officially adopted. It was designated a village shrine on February 24 and a district shrine on November 11. The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線|qq=draft}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Omonoimi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{wikidata link|Q11462862}}{{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[co:commons:Category:Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]] [[ja:小物忌神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sakata City]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

The shrine burned down in 1899 but was rebuilt by 1901, earning prefectural shrine status in 1902. === Timeline === * Emperor Keikō's reign - Recorded by Takenouchi no Sukune. * 878 - Promoted to Lower Fifth Rank, Seventh Court Rank. * 880 - Promoted to Upper Fifth Rank. * 1091 - Minamoto no Yoshiie prayed for victory. * February 24, 1876 - Designated a village shrine. * November 11, 1876 - Designated a district shrine, affiliated with Chōkai-san Ōmonoimi Shrine. * March 25, 1902 - Designated a prefectural shrine. == Transportation == * Approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|JR|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|ja|羽越本線|qq=draft}} Sagoshi Station. == Notes == {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == References == * Yūgi Saeki, ''Complete Works on Shinto, Volume 1,'' Kōten Kōkyūsho, October 1906 (Reprinted by Shibunkaku in 1971). * Kenichi Tanigawa (ed.), ''Japanese Gods and Sacred Sites, Volume 12: Tōhoku and Hokkaidō,'' Hakusuisha, June 1984. * Shinto Taikei Compilation Committee (ed.), ''Shinto Taikei: Shrines of Dewa Province,'' March 1990. * Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Association 50th Anniversary Editorial Committee, ''Yamagata Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,'' April 2000. == See Also == {{Commons|Category:Omonoimi-jinja}} * {{ill|Omonoimi Shrine (Sakata City Tobishima)|ja|小物忌神社 (酒田市飛島)|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.omonoimi.com/ Official Site of Omonoimi Shrine] {{Omonoimi Faith}} {{wikidata link|Q11462862|co|commons:Category:Omonoimi-jinja, Yamadate|ja|小物忌神社}}{{translated page|ja|小物忌神社|version=83984308|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sakata City]] [[Category:Chokaisan Faith]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakata City]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Omonoimi Faith]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
